summary: Direct batch error CSV inspection is blocked by VPC Service Controls from this environment.

findings: Every sampled batch_errors CSV returned AccessDeniedException 403 Request is prohibited by organization's policy, so root-cause detail must come from Pizza rows and Bifrost/LiveRamp logs unless accessed from an allowed environment.

summary: Cloud Logging shows representative blocked batches failing before export send because LiveRamp API validation calls timed out or returned HTTP 500.

findings: Batch 598d8ff9... failed validate_credentials after repeated api.liveramp.com read timeouts and moved EXPORT_QUEUED->EXPORT_ERROR; batch cc5777bd... failed validate_credentials after repeated LiveRamp HTTP 500 responses and moved EXPORT_QUEUED->EXPORT_ERROR. Batches 9cb6515f... and 5b97c8de... later completed after validation/send, but their export runs still show failed_batch_count>0 in Pizza, so run-level export_error remains unresolved.

summary: Refreshed export preflight for consolidated Albertsons LiveRamp case; all 12 alert checks remain blocked on export_error.

findings: Preflight evaluated 12 checks, skipped 0 unavailable envs, resolved=false, monitor_reason=no_monitoring_checks; first Pizza request retried once then succeeded.

summary: Completed targeted Cloud Logging sweep for all 15 blocked Pizza run evaluations in the consolidated Albertsons LiveRamp export_error case.

commands: gcloud logging read 'resource.type="k8s_container" resource.labels.namespace_name="bifrost" jsonPayload.export_run_id=<export_run_id> severity>=WARNING' --project=gl-client-albertsons --format=json
findings: Batch-id sweep: direct error-file batch ids for 12535, 12468, 12495, and 11579 move to EXPORT_ERROR during validate_credentials after repeated LiveRamp timeout or HTTP 500 responses; several other Pizza error-file batch ids reach EXPORT_FINISHED, so the failed run cause is in sibling batches or aggregate state.

Run-level sweep: June 4, June 5, June 15, and June 19 runs contain sibling batch failures from Bifrost SFTP send permission errors in addition to LiveRamp API timeout/HTTP 500/invalid credential validation failures. June 21 audience 12506 shows LiveRamp HTTP 500s on destinations and deliveries; audiences 12468 and 12495 fail on their direct error-file batches.

This consolidated case should be split by actual failure mode before state transition: SFTP send permission errors versus LiveRamp API validation/activation failures. It is not ready for monitoring or resolved because preflight still reports all 12 checks blocked.
log_queries: project=gl-client-albertsons namespace=bifrost batch_ids=15 Pizza error_file_uri batch ids; export_run_ids=15 blocked run evaluations; bounded windows around run created_at

summary: Deferred structural split after the log sweep because the CLI splits by alert, while several alert facts contain mixed run-level failure modes.

findings: Safe split candidate: a LiveRamp API-only subgroup appears to include audiences 12535, 11579, 12468, 12495, and 12506. The remaining alerts include SFTP send permission failures and/or mixed sibling-batch failures, so they need a separately named mixed/SFTP case or per-run evidence before splitting.
No state transition applied: all 12 checks remain blocked; no durable owner/customer communication evidence exists for waiting; monitoring/resolved would be unsupported.

summary: Follow-up on LiveRamp invalid_grant credential/token rejection: later LiveRamp calls succeeded, so the credential rejection was not a persistent endpoint-wide failure.

commands: gcloud logging read 'resource.type="k8s_container" resource.labels.namespace_name="bifrost" jsonPayload.export_run_id=<run> (invalid_grant or successful activation evidence)' --project=gl-client-albertsons --format=json
findings: Audience 12399 had invalid_grant/token rejection on 2026-05-28 around 02:07 UTC, but later batches in the same run reached SYNCED/DELIVERED and EXPORT_FINISHED.

Other invalid-credential classified events on 2026-06-05 and 2026-06-19 were also surrounded by successful LiveRamp activation/status calls in the same export runs. The affected Pizza checks remain blocked, but the current evidence points to mixed per-batch failures rather than an unresolved global LiveRamp credential outage.

summary: Checked whether audience 12399 alert can be resolved after the invalid_grant credential symptom disappeared; current export check is still blocked.

commands: bun run oncall-triage check-exports cases --filter group.id=260622-albertsons_6-live-ramp-export-error --filter alert.audience=12399
findings: Fresh check evaluated Q1E4EUPF9HDZNA/Q3VSW000L0VZB7 for audience 12399 and wrote state=blocked with export_error on both checked runs.
Do not mark resolved: the invalid_grant symptom was transient, but the PagerDuty alert is for export_error and Pizza still reports the export runs blocked.

summary: Detailed latest-error check for audience 12495 batch 16dbfa53: LiveRamp returned HTTP 500 from the destinations validation endpoint after two read timeouts.

commands: gcloud logging read 'resource.type="k8s_container" resource.labels.namespace_name="bifrost" jsonPayload.batch_id="16dbfa53-4ade-4418-abed-7a48ddfd7323" timestamp >= "2026-06-21T01:14:00Z" timestamp < "2026-06-21T01:16:00Z"' --project=gl-client-albertsons --format=json
findings: Retry sequence: attempts 1-2 were ReadTimeouts to api.liveramp.com with read timeout=10; attempts 3-4 were HTTPError 500 Server Error for https://api.liveramp.com/activation/v2/destinations?limit=1.

Bifrost logged the failure in LiveRampActivationClient._execute_request / validate_credentials, then marked validation_failed, moved the batch from EXPORT_QUEUED to EXPORT_ERROR, and aggregate export state from NO_BATCHES to EXPORT_ERROR.
The available log payload does not include a LiveRamp response body or request id beyond the HTTP status and URL.

summary: Rechecked audience 12495 latest LiveRamp export_error on 2026-06-23; it is still the newest Pizza row and still blocked.

commands: bun run oncall-triage check-exports cases --filter group.id=260622-albertsons_6-live-ramp-export-error --filter alert.audience=12495
findings: Fresh check evaluated Q1S0Q38FOEN2XY/Q0491BXP5ERJ3Q and wrote state=blocked with blocker export_error.
Updated check artifact shows the 2026-06-21 run remains export_error with failed_batch_count=1; the 2026-06-14 checked runs are healthy/export_finished.
Direct Pizza listing returned 9 rows and the 2026-06-21 01:14:30 UTC row is still newest for audience 12495, so there is no later same-audience success in Pizza to supersede this alert.

summary: Prepared handoff links for latest audience 12495 failure: Vortex impersonation URL and targeted Cloud Logging query for batch 16dbfa53.
